_comment: The single source of truth for which Swiss Ephemeris executables Orbit ships,
where they live, and what they must hash to. Nothing here is a secret.
Checksums are real and verified — never use placeholders. Regenerate with:

source.licence: Dual-licensed: AGPL-3.0 or a paid Astrodienst commercial licence.
source.licenceStatus: UNRESOLVED. No licence has been selected, purchased, or documented for a publicly reachable Orbit deployment. See docs/deployment/swiss-ephemeris-licensing.md. Keeping this repository private does not by itself resolve the obligations of a public hosted service.

darwin-arm64.origin: Pre-existing in the repository since the initial Orbit extraction. Built for Apple Silicon macOS.
darwin-arm64.purpose: Local development on the owner's Mac.

linux-x64.format: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, x86-64, statically linked
linux-x64.origin: Built from the official Astrodienst source at tag v2.10.03 (commit 175e1fc) inside a throwaway debian:bookworm-slim linux/amd64 container using the upstream Makefile's `swetests` (static) target. No third-party or mirror binary was downloaded.
linux-x64.purpose: Vercel Node functions and any Linux x64 build or test environment. Statically linked so it does not depend on the host glibc version — verified running on busybox, which ships no glibc at all.

unsupported.darwin-x64: Practical to add, but no verified artifact was built and no Intel Mac is available to test on. Not added rather than claimed.
unsupported.linux-arm64: Practical to add (this machine can build it natively), but Vercel functions are x64 and nothing in the project needs it today. Not added rather than shipped untested.
